title: Sacramento, CA – Sacramento – Today, Assemblymember Cecilia Aguiar-Curry (D – Winters) introduced AB 525 to help small and new businesses stay open when faced with unexpected tax b

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The tax code is complex for anyone, but for a new business it can be overwhelming and lead to unintended mistakes.",
    "Few small businesses have accounting specialists, so the responsibility for tax-related matters falls on the owner.",
    "The Offer in Compromise Program provides entrepreneurs with a second chance if they find out that they’ve made a mistake calculating or reporting their taxes.",
    "The Board of Equalization operates the Offer in Compromise Program, which gives it the authority to compromise on a tax plan with a business owner who may be unable to pay unexpected business taxes in full.",
    "The end of the Offer in Compromise Program would devastate small business owners trying to stay afloat.",
    "The closure of small businesses would cause an unnecessary hit to California’s economy.",
    "Since 2009, the BOE has accepted compromises from 42 businesses that remain open today and continue to pay their sales and use taxes properly and on time.",
    "The total amount from these 42 businesses contributes an additional $14.3 million to the benefit of state and local governments."
  ]
}
```
